title: Magu District Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2002
title_en: Magu District Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2002

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: This instrument may be cited as the Magu District Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2002, and shall come into operation on the date of its publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: This Instrument shall apply throughout the area of jurisdiction of the Magu District Council.

provisions.title: 3 Interpretation
provisions.content: (1) In the Instrument unless the context otherwise requires:— “Act” means the Local Government (District Authorities) Act; 1982 “Council Health Management Team” means a technical health management organ of the district established under section 14 of this instrument; “Region Health Management Team” means a team of Health experts in-charge of managing health services in the Region; “Hospital Governing Committee” means a committee established under section 17 of this instrument; “Council health Management Team” means a team of head of sections or units of a District Health Department. “Health Centre Committee” means a committee established under section 27(1) of this Instrument; “Health Centre Management Team” means a team of head of sections or units of a Health centre; “Ward Development Committee” means a committee established under section 37 of the Local Government (District Authorities) Act; “Dispensary Committee” means a committee established under section 38 of this Instrument; “Dispensary Management Team” means a managerial organ of a dispensary; “Dispensary” means a primary health care facility, which offers outpatient services, including maternal and child health services, and may have up to four bed for observation of patients for not more than 24 hours; “Council Hospital” means a District Hospital “Health Centre” means a health facility usually located at the Ward level, offering outpatient and inpatient health care services including maternal and child health services with a bed capacity of about 15 beds; “District Hospital” means a level 1-hospital establishment in the district empowered to supervise all health services in the district; “Designated District Hospital” means a voluntary agency level 1-hospital establishment empowered to act as the district hospital on contractual agreement with the Government; “Health Facility” means and includes a hospital, health centre, medical and dental clinics, dispensary, pharmacy, health laboratory, diagnostic centre, maternity home and nursing home and includes such other facilities established or organised for purposes of delivering health services; “District Medical Officer” means a medical practitioner who is the officer in charge of health services in the district; “Public health facility” means a health facility that is owned and managed by the Government to address people’s needs; “Minister” means the Minister responsible for Local Government. “Non Governmental Organization (NGO) health facility” means a health facility owned, financed and run by an Organization or an Association which is normally organising charitable non profit making private services; and operates within laid down regulations of the Government; “Private health facility” means a health facility owned and managed by private individual or corporate body mainly for profit making; “Board” means the Council Health Service Board established under section 4(1) of this Instrument; “Council” means the Magu District Council established under section 5 of the Local Government (District Authorities) Act 1982; “Ward Health Committee” means a Committee established under section 35 of this Instrument. [Act No. 7 of 1982] (2) Word connoting the masculine gender includes female gender.

provisions.title: Establishment of the Board
provisions.content: (1) There is hereby established a Board, to be known as the Council Health Service Board. The offices of the Board shall be within the Offices of the council.

provisions.title: Composition of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall be composed of the following members namely; (a) four community service users of the health services of whom at least two shall be female; (b) one representative each from a non for profit voluntary agency and a private for profit health care facility, appointed by the Council from amongst health care facilities which have entered into a service agreement with the Board; (c) the Chairman of the Council Social Services Committee; (d) the Council Planning Officer; (e) the District Medical Officer who shall be the Secretary to the Board; (f) medical Officer incharge of the Council Hospital; (g) one representative from the Regional Health Management Team. (2) The Chairman shall be elected from amongst members specified under paragraphs (a) and (b) of subsection (1) of this section; (3) Member specified under paragraphs (a), (b) and (c) of other than members specified under paragraphs (d), (e), (f) and (g) of subsection (1), shall have voting rights in any decision of the Board. (4) The Board may invite any person to attend and speak on any matter, or participate in any other way, at any meeting of the Board, but any person so invited shall not be entitled to vote at the meeting.

provisions.title: Tenure of office and mode of selection
provisions.content: (1) Members of the Board shall hold office for a period of three years from the date of their nomination and cease to be a member after that period, but may be eligible for re-nomination for another term of three years only. (2) At the end of the tenure of office of the current Board, the Council Director shall announce to the Public, the open vacancy which require competent personnel to fill them by filling special application form and submit them to the Council. (3) If any vacancy occurs in the membership of the Board by the reason of death resignation, permanent incapacity or any other reason, another person shall be nominated by the Council to fill up the vacancy and person nominated shall hold office for the remaining term, provided that nothing in this section shall be constructed as allowing to nominate another person if the remaining period is less than six months. (4) The person so nominated, as per subsection (3) above shall be eligible for re-election. (5) Selection of elected candidates shall be made by the Full Council through the Social Services Committee and the Planning and Finance Committee after being scrutinized by the Council Management Team. (6) The Board shall be approved by the full Council and inaugurated under the witness of the District Commissioner. (7) When the Board is dissolved, all members shall lose their posts.

provisions.title: Meeting of the Council Health Services Board
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of subsection (2) of this subsection, the Board shall ordinarily meet quarterly in every calendar year and the chairman shall convene all meetings of the Board. (2) The Chairman may convene a special/extra-ordinary meeting of the Board on a request in writing signed by not less than one third of the total number of members of the Board when the need arises.

provisions.title: Quorum and voting at meetings
provisions.content: (1) A one-half of the total number of members in office shall constitute a quorum at any meeting of the Board. (2) A decision of the majority of members present and voting at a meeting of the Board shall be deemed to be a decision of the Board. (3) The Board shall regulate its meetings. (4) Every member of the Board shall have one vote and in event of equality of votes, the Chairman of the meeting shall have a second or casting vote in addition to his deliberative vote.

provisions.title: Qualifications of members
provisions.content: (1) A person shall be qualified to be a member of the Board if: (a) he is a Tanzanian citizen; (b) for representatives of NGOs and Voluntary agency; the question of citizenship shall not apply; (c) he has attained the age of twenty five years and not older than seventy years provided that the subject is of sound body and mind; and (d) applicants shall have secondary education and above. (e) he does not suffer from any mental disease. (f) he has not been convicted of any criminal offence. (2) A councilor or an employee of the Council whether elected or not, save for the Chairman of the Social Services Committee, shall not be allowed to be a voting member of the Board. (3) A member who, without leave of absence or the prior written consent of the chairman absents himself from three consecutive meeting of the Board, shall be liable to be discontinue as a member of the Board.

provisions.title: Roles and functions of the Board
provisions.content: The roles, functions and responsibilities of the Board shall be as follows:— (a) to ensure that the population receives appropriate and affordable, promotive, preventive, curative and rehabilitative health care services,; (b) to discuss, amend health plans, budget and submit to the Council for approval; (c) to receive, analyse and approve implementation reports from the Council Health Management Team; (d) to identify, mobilise and solicit financial resources for running Council health services; (e) to mobilise adequate resources that will ensure effective, efficient and equitable access to health services in the district; (f) to support Council Health Management Team in managing and administering health resources; (g) to prepare and recommend on a Council wide health plan and budget of the committee responsible for health services; (h) to promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable logistics and supply systems; (i) to develop, supervise and manage health services in the district; (j) to advise on human resources recruitment, training, selection in the employment of Health staff.

provisions.title: Relationship of the Board with
provisions.content: Relationship of the Board with community, stakeholders and other authorities shall be as follows:— (a) The Board shall ensure full involvement of the local community in priority identification, planning, financial contribution, monitoring and evaluation of health services delivery; (b) With Non Governmental Organizations, private providers and donors:— (i) Members representing the Voluntary Agency, Non Governmental Organizations and private providers in the Board will facilitate the Relationship; (ii) The Board will require a proof from Council Health Management Team that other stakeholders have been involved in the planning process; (iii) The Board shall solicit for funds from all various sources including Councils, Voluntary Agency, community and donors. (c) Regional Health Management Team shall provide technical support and advisory role; (d) The ministries responsible for Local Government and Health shall give technical support to the Board by formulating Policies, Regulatory framework and monitoring of the Board’s activities.

provisions.title: Accountability of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall be accountable to the Council, whereby (a) The Council Health Plans quarterly, annual technical and financial reports shall be reviewed by Social Services Committee and Planning Committee and approved by full Council; (b) The Board shall conduct its activities in such a way that is shall maximize the efficiency of the Council and its existing organs without Council jeopardising the Board’s autonomy. (c) The Council Health Management Team shall be a technical structure for implementing the decisions passed by the Board. (d) In respect of Council Social Services Committee. (i) All plans and progress reports shall pass, therefore, this ComMmittee will facilitate collaboration; (ii) Close collaboration with other sectors will be actively searched by the Board towards the implementation of joint efforts in priority areas within the council. (2) If the Board fails to fulfill its expected obligations as stipulated by the Relevant Council Instrument, the Council shall reprimand or dis­solve the Board and instruct arrangement for another selection within two months. (3) When the Board is dissolved all the members shall loose their posts. (4) For the period which the Board stands dissolved the functions and powers of the Board shall rest in the Council health Management team.

provisions.title: Source and management of funds
provisions.content: (1) The sources of funds for the Board shall be:— (a) Community contribution; (b) Community Health Fund; (c) Cost sharing and user charges fees - from the facility; (d) Council Grants; (e) Donors and partners donations; (f) Central Government grants; (g) Other sources. (2) There shall be opened and maintained account No. 6 in respect of health service resources in which all health service revenue managed by the Board shall be deposited and withdrawn according to local Government financial regulations, and signatories for this account shall be as prescribed by the Council from time to time. (3) (i) The Board shall maintain a special account for the purposes of Community Health Fund iniatives. This account will maintain revenues from sales of Council Health Fund cards and collection of user fees from Council Health facilities. Signatories of this Fund account will be as stipulated in the By-laws establishing Community Health Fund. (4) The Council shall appoint two accountants who shall oversee health accounts under the District Medical Officer. (5) The District Medical Officer shall initiate/originate all payments. (6) Both accounts shall be audited following prevailing Local Government regulations. (7) By-laws for Community Health Fund shall be formed for the purposes of management of its activities in the Council.

provisions.title: Establishment of Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: There shall be established a Council Health Management Team.

provisions.title: Composition of the Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: (1) The Council Health Management Team shall consist of:— (a) District Medical Officer; (b) District Health Secretary; (c) District Nursing Officer; (d) District Health Officer; (e) District Pharmacist; (f) District Medical Laboratory Technologist; (g) District Dental surgeon; (h) Medical Officer in charge of the Council Hospital

provisions.title: Roles and functions of the Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: The Roles and functions of the Council Health Management Team shall be:— (a) to prepare comprehensive Council health plans in line with the Council health needs and National District Health Planning Guidelines; (b) to ensure that health activities are implemented by hospitals, health centres, dispensaries, community owned resource persons and the communities as per comprehensive district health plan; (c) to ensure the provision of transport, drugs, vaccines, medical supplies, equipment and reagents to hospital, health centres and dispensaries; (d) to respond to epidemics, emergencies and plan for control and preventive measures accordingly; (e) to carry out supportive supervision to district health staff at all levels in the district; (f) to ensure that quality health services are provide in line with national standards; (g) to ensure health legislations, and professional ethics, and codes of conduct are adhered to in the district; (h) to ensure that data collection, analysis, utilisation and feedback are used by health workers to plan and implement proper interventions; (i) to identify priority areas, organise and conduct operational research in the district; (j) to compile and analyse quarterly and annually all technical and financial reports and submit them to the Council Health Service Board and Regional Health Management Team; (k) to organise meetings of all key health partners operating in the district in order to identify and coordinate all health activities; (l) to organise and support outreach health services; (m) to monitor and evaluate implementation health activities in the district.

provisions.title: Establishment of Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: There shall be established for each hospital under the management and supervision of the Council, or designated hospital, a Committee to be referred to as a “Hospital Governing Committee”.

provisions.title: Composition of Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Hospital Governing Committee shall consist of: Elected members who shall vote and non-vote members as follows:- (Vote members); (a) three persons of reputable character appointed from amongst the members of the community receiving health services or regis­tered to receive services from the hospital; (b) two persons appointed by the Council from members of the Health Centre Committee and Dispensary Committee within the area of jurisdiction of the Council; (c) one person appointed by the Council from voluntary agencies providing services with the area of jurisdiction of the Council. (d) one person appointed by the Council from private for profit health facility providing services within the area of jurisdiction of the council. (Non-Vote members); (e) one member from the Council Health Service Board; (f) Medical Officer in charge of the Council hospital who shall be a secretary. (g) one representative from the office of the DMO (2) At least one third of the members shall be women. (3) Hospital Governing Committee may invite persons who are not their members to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons shall not take part in the final decisions of the Committee. (4) the Committee shall elect its chairman from amongst the members who can vote in a meeting.

provisions.title: Qualification of Members of the hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: Member of the Hospital Governing Committee shall have the following qualifications: (a) be a citizen of the United Republic of Tanzania; (b) be 25 years of age and not older than sixty five years; (c) the level of his education shall be primary school or above, and can read and write in Kiswahili; (d) he does not hold any political post.

provisions.title: Procedure for selecting Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provision of sub-section (2) and (3) of this section, all members of the Hospital Governing Committee shall be selected in accordance with principles of meritocracy and transparency. (2) The position of membership to the Hospital Governing Committee shall be advertised in the area of jurisdiction of the Council and such advertisement shall call upon qualified persons the Council Director. (3) Selection of elected candidates shall be made by Full Council through the Social Services Committee after being recommended by Council Management Team. (4) The most suitable applicants shall be members of the Hospital Governing Committee.

provisions.title: Tenure of office
provisions.content: (1) The tenure of office of a member of the Hospital Governing Committee shall be three years and at the end of that period a member can be eligible for re-appointment for another term of three years only. (2) If the Hospital Governing Committee fails to fulfil its expected obligations, the Council shall reprimand or dissolve the committee and arrange for an immediate election and selection as the case may be.

provisions.title: Meetings of the Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Hospital Governing Committee shall hold ordinary meetings for the transaction of its business at least once in every month at places and dates and such times as the Committee may decide. (2) The chairman of the Hospital Governing Committee may after consultation with the Secretary of the Committee convene a special or extra-ordinary meeting on such time as it may deem necessary; (3) Notice of every meeting shall be sent to the members not less than two days before the date appointed for the meeting, notifying members in writing of the place, date and time of the meeting and of the business proposed to be transacted at the meeting.

provisions.title: Functions of Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) The functions, roles and responsibilities of the Hospital Governing Committee shall be: (a) to oversee proper management or resources of the hospital; (b) receive, discuss and pass proposals for hospital plans and budgets and submit to the Council for approval through Council Health Service Board; (c) receive and discuss the implementation reports prepared by Hospital Management Team; (d) identify mobilized and solicit financial resources for the financing of the hospital services; (e) liaise with other health committees and partners in health provision and promotion; (f) to make recommendations on selection, employment training and deployment to the relevant body of the Council so as to ensure proper, effective and efficient human resource disposition of the hospital; (g) provide feed back to the community on hospital operations and development plans.

provisions.title: Reporting and accountability
provisions.content: (1) The Hospital Governing Committee shall report to and be answerable and accountable to the Council through the Council Health Service Board. (2) If the Hospital fails to perform its functions, the Board shall reprimand or dissolve the Committee and arrange for another selection within two Months. (3) When the Governing Committee is dissolved, all the members shall lose their posts. (4) For the period during which the Governing Committee stands dissolved, the functions and powers of Governing Committee shall rest in the Hospital Management Team.

provisions.title: Relationship of Hospital Governing Committee with other Committee or authorities
provisions.content: The Hospital Governing Committee shall, for the purposes of better Management, liaise with other health committees or providers for purposes of creating an integrated health services delivery within the establishment System of health services in the Council.

provisions.title: Sources and management of funds
provisions.content: (1) The source of revenue for the Hospital Governing Committee shall consist of: (a) Such amounts that will be allocated to the hospital in the Councils Budget and appropriated by the Council by way of grant through Council Health Service Board; (b) Health service user charges or part of such charges assigned by Council Health Service Board to form part of resources of the Hospital; (c) Such amount which may be received by way of grant or Subvention, donation from the Council, Central Government Institutions or private individuals; (d) Any other moneys that may lawfully be received by the Board from any other source not specified in this Instrument. (2) There shall be a single hospital account which shall follow prevailing local Government financial regulations. (3) The District Medical Officer will assign one of his accountants to the Hospital. Who shall be responsible for the operation of the hospital account, which shall be under the supervision of the Medical Officer In-charge. (4) The Medical Officer In-charge shall initiate and originate all payments regarding hospital activities. (5) The Hospital account shall be audited following Local Government regulations.

provisions.title: Establishment of a Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) There shall be established for each Health Centre, a Health Centre Committee which shall be composed of:— (a) three members appointed one each from a ward within the area. (b) one member appointed by the Health Committee from the Dispensary Committee (On Rotational basis) at its first meeting. (c) one member appointed by Private for profit Health facility in the area. (d) the Officer in-charge of the Health Centre who shall be the secretary; (e) one member representing private not-for Profit Health facility. (2) Health Centre Committee may invite other persons who are not members of the Health Centre Committee to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons shall not take part in the final decisions of the committee. (3) At least one third of the members shall be women. (4) The chairman of the committee shall be elected by the members from amongst themselves provided that it shall not include the member specified in paragraph (d) of this section.

provisions.title: Qualification for membership
provisions.content: (1) A person shall be qualified for appointment as a member of a Health Centre Committee if: (a) he is a citizen of United Republic of Tanzania; (b) he has attained the age of twenty one years and not older than sixty five years; (c) he is ordinarily resident within the area of jurisdiction of the Health Centre. (d) the level of his education is Primary School or above and can read and write in Kiswahili; (e) he does not hold any political post. (f) he is not suffering from any mental disease. (g) he has not been convicted of any criminal offence. (2) A member who without leave of absence or prior written consent of the Chairman of the Committee absent himself from three consecutive meetings of the committee shall be liable to be discontinued as a member of the Health Centre Committee.

provisions.title: Tenure of office of members and mode of selection
provisions.content: (1) The term of office of a member shall be three years and they shall retire on the third anniversary of the date on which they assumed their offices and if not re-appointed for another term, newly appointed members shall come into office on that day to fill their places. (2) The Board shall announce to the public the open vacancy calling upon qualified persons to fill them. (3) Any appointed member may resign by giving a written notice of one month to the Chairman of the Committee. (4) If any vacancy occurs in the membership of a Health Committee by reason of death, resignation or any other reason, the appropriate authority shall fill the vacant post and the appointed member shall hold office only for the remainder of the term of his predecessor and shall be eligible for re-appointment. (5) If the Health Centre Committee fails to fulfil its expected obligations, the Council shall repremand or dissolve the committee and arrange for another selection within two months. (6) Where the Committee stands dissolved, the powers and functions of the committee shall rest in the Health Centre Management Team.

provisions.title: Meetings of Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Committee shall hold ordinary meetings for the transaction of its business at least once in every three months at a place, dates and such times as it may decide provided that the Committee may convene a special meeting as it may deem fit. (2) The Chairman of the Committee may convene a special or extraordinary meeting of the Committee after receives a written request to that effect signed by not less than one third of all the members of the Committee stating clearly the purposes for such a meeting to be convened. (3) The Secretary shall, not less than twenty-four hours before the time appointed for the holding of a meeting of a Committee, notify every member in writing of the place, day and time of the meeting and of the business proposed to be transacted at the meeting.

provisions.title: Functions of Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) It shall be the duty of every Committee of a Health Centre:— (a) to receive and discuss implementation reports prepared by the Health Centre Management Team; (b) to liaise with the Council Health Board and other partners in Health provision and promotion in its area. (c) to promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable supply of drugs and logistic systems; (d) to advise and recommend to the Council Health Service Board on human resources development in terms of recruitment, training, deployment and motivation at the Health Centre level; (e) to facilitate the Health Centre Management Team in planning and managing Community based health initiatives within its catchment area in the context of the Ward Development Plan; (f) to promote availability of adequate Health Centre infrastructure; (g) to oversee the availability of essential supplies of drugs, reagents and Medical equipment. (2) The Health Centre Committee shall be responsible and accountable for its day to day functions to the Council Health Service Board.

provisions.title: Funds and source of revenue
provisions.content: (1) Funds and sources of revenue of a Health Centre shall constitute the following:— (a) Community contributions; (b) donor or partner donations; (c) Government grants; (d) Council grants; (e) any other source legally acquired. (2) There shall be a special account at a Bank recognised by the Council where all the funds for the Health Centre shall be deposited. This will be determined by the amount of funds in circulation. (3) There shall be two signatories for cheque of Health Centre as follows: the officer in-charge of a health centre shall be category A, and one appointed user form the committee of a health Centre shall be category B. (4) There shall be in every health Centre a strong room and a safe for the custody of Money of a Health Centre. (5) The health Centre account shall be audited in accordance with the Local Government financial regulations. (6) The Health Centre shall send quarterly, bi annual and annual progress report to the Council Health Board, to be presented before the full council meetings.

provisions.title: Relations with other sectors
provisions.content: (1) The Health Centre Committee shall, in performance of its functions: (a) make sure that the community needs are addressed; (b) solicit funds from various sources; (c) keep and maintain a Health Fund contributor’s register; (d) develop and strengthen partnership for collaboration, coordination and integration with other sectors in the area; (e) make sure that the Dispensary Committee is represented in the Health Centre Committee; (f) make sure that the Health Centre Committee is represented in the Council Health Service Board. (2) The Council Health Management Team shall give technical advice to the Health Centre Management Team. (3) The Health Centre Committee shall be accountable and answer­able to the Board.

provisions.title: Establishment of the Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: There shall be established a Ward Health Committee.

provisions.title: 35 Composition the Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Ward Health Committee shall be comprised of:— (a) the Councillor of the respective ward who shall not be appointed as a Chairman of the committee; (b) Councelors of the Council who are resident in the Ward. (c) the Ward Executive Officer; (d) one headteacher from primary schools located in the ward who shall be appointed by the Ward Development Committee; (e) two members from the community which, one of whom shall be a female; (f) a clinical officer or an assistant Clinical Officer in charge of a health care facility, on rotation basis who shall be the secretary to the committee; (g) one member appointed by the Ward Development Committee from amongst persons proposed by the villages within the area of that ward; and (h) one representative appointed by the Ward Development Committee from a community based organisation. (2) The chairman of the Committee shall be elected by the members from amongst members specified in paragraphs (e), (g) and (h) of this section.

provisions.title: Function of Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: (1) Ward Health Committee shall have the following functions:— (a) to mobilize the community to be members of the Community Health Fund; (b) to prepare the list of members and monitor the number of members in the community; (c) to monitor the level of contributions and user-fee revenues; (d) to review Community Health Fund’s Operations, make recommendations and take remedial actions. (e) to initiate and coordinate comprehensive community health plans: and mobilize the Ward Community on issues partaining to improvement of Environmental Health. (f) to organize general meeting of members of the Community Health Fund. (g) to do any other thing as may be directed by the Ward Development Committee. (2) The Ward health committee shall be accountable to the Ward Development Committee.

provisions.title: Establishment of Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: There is hereby established a Committee to be known as a Dispensary Committee.

provisions.title: Composition of a Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: (1) A Dispensary Committee shall be composed of the following members:— (a) Selected members who shall be vote members as follows:— (i) three members from community dispensary users; (ii) one member representing the private not for profit health facilities, (iii) one member representing the private for profit health facilities. (b) non-vote members as follows:— (i) one representative from a Ward Development Committee; (ii) one representative from a Village Government Committee; and (iii) In-charge of the dispensary who shall be the Secretary. (iv) Two members from all the sub-Villages in the area to be appointed on rotational basis. (2) The Dispensary Committee may invite persons who are not their members to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons shall not take part in the final decisions of the Committee. (3) At least one third of the members shall be women. (4) The chairman of the committee shall be elected by the members from amongst members specified in paragraph (i) of this section.

provisions.title: Mode of selection and tenure of office
provisions.content: (1) Members of a Dispensary Committee shall be selected through the following procedure:— (a) Ward Executive Officer shall advertise the vacant posts for representation health facilities users; (b) The list of selected candidates shall be forwarded to the Ward Management Team for scrutiny before being forwarded to Ward Development Committee which shall do the final selection and approval. (2) Tenure of office for Dispensary Committee members shall be three years but may seek re-election for another term of three years only. (3) If the Health Centre Committee fails to perform its functions, the Board shall reprimand or dissolve the Committee and shall arrange for another selection within two months. (4) For the period during which the Committee stands dissolved, the Functions and powers of the committee shall rest in the Dispensary Management Team.

provisions.title: Meetings of Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: (1) A Dispensary Committee shall meet quarterly, but it may convene an extra-ordinary meeting when need arise. (2) Fifty percent of the selected members together with a Chairman or Secretary shall form a quorum for any meeting of a Dispensary Committee.

provisions.title: Qualification members
provisions.content: (1) A person shall be eligible to be a member of a Dispensary Committee if:— (a) he has attained the age of 21 and not older than sixty five years. (b) he has completed Primary Education or above and know how to read and write Kiswahili. (c) he is a Tanzanian and ordinarily a resident of The Council. However the condition of the citizenship shall not apply to a person who is working in a voluntary health facility. (d) he is representing the users of a dispensary. (e) he is mentally fit; (f) he has not been convicted of any criminal offence. (2) A member who without leave of absence or the prior written consent of the Chairman of the Committee absent himself from three consecutive meetings of the Committee shall be liable to be discontinued as a member of the Dispensary Committee.

provisions.title: Roles and functions of a Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: (1) A Dispensary Committee shall have the following roles and functions:— (a) to ensure that the population in its area of jurisdiction receives appropriate and affordable health services; (b) receive, discuss and pass the dispensary plans and budget; (c) receive the implementation reports prepared by Dispensary Management Team; (d) identify and solicit financial resources for running dispensary services; (e) liaise with other health boards and partners in health provision and promotion; (f) promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable supply and logistic system; (g) advise and recommend on human resources concerning recruitment, training, selection and deployment to relevant authorities; (h) assist and facilitate the dispensary Management Team in planning and managing the community based health initiatives within its catchment area in the context of the Ward Development Plan; (i) submit quarterly, biannual and annual reports to the Council Health Service Board; and (j) do any relevant activities as may be directed by the Ward Development Committee.

provisions.title: Relationship of Dispensary Committee with relevant Authorities
provisions.content: (1) For the better performance and efficiency in its day to day activities; a Dispensary Committee shall liaise with the following:— (a) Community by ensuring that the Community contribute members to its Committee to ensure that community needs are adequately addressed and facilitate flow of information from both ways. (b) Council Health Management Team which shall give technical advice to it; (c) The Council Health Service Board by being represented in it on a rotational basis to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (d) The Dispensary Management Team to ensure that the Team imple­ment Council’s decisions; (e) The Health Centre Committee by being represented in order to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed. (f) Other stakeholders by soliciting funds, develop and strengthen partnerships for collaboration, co-ordination and intergration. (2) The committee shall submit its reports to the Board.

provisions.title: Sources of funds
provisions.content: Source of fund for the Dispensary Committee shall include but not be limited to the following:- (I) community contribution; (II) Community Health Fund; (III) Council’s Grants; (IV) funds from donors or partners; (V) Central Government; and (vi) other sources legally acquired.

provisions.title: Board to inspect
provisions.content: The Board shall have a right to inspect at any time the conditions of the operations of the health services and maintenance of Health Facilities, and shall report the matter to the full council. definitions.definition: the Local Government (District Authorities) Act
a technical health management organ of the district established under section 14 of this instrument
a team of Health experts in-charge of managing health services in the Region
a committee established under section 17 of this instrument
a team of head of sections or units of a Health centre
a committee established under section 37 of the Local Government (District Authorities) Act
a committee established under section 38 of this Instrument
a managerial organ of a dispensary
a primary health care facility, which offers outpatient services, including maternal and child health services, and may have up to four bed for observation of patients for not more than 24 hours

a District Hospital “Health Centre” means a health facility usually located at the Ward level, offering outpatient and inpatient health care services including maternal and child health services with a bed capacity of about 15 beds
a level 1-hospital establishment in the district empowered to supervise all health services in the district
a voluntary agency level 1-hospital establishment empowered to act as the district hospital on contractual agreement with the Government

and includes a hospital, health centre, medical and dental clinics, dispensary, pharmacy, health laboratory, diagnostic centre, maternity home and nursing home and includes such other facilities established or organised for purposes of delivering health services
a medical practitioner who is the officer in charge of health services in the district
a health facility that is owned and managed by the Government to address people’s needs

the Minister responsible for Local Government. “Non Governmental Organization (NGO) health facility” means a health facility owned, financed and run by an Organization or an Association which is normally organising charitable non profit making private services
a health facility owned and managed by private individual or corporate body mainly for profit making
the Council Health Service Board established under section 4(1) of this Instrument
the Magu District Council established under section 5 of the Local Government (District Authorities) Act 1982
a Committee established under section 35 of this Instrument. [Act No. 7 of 1982] (2) Word connoting the masculine gender includes female gender.
